summary: claw_messenger postinstall silently global-installs unrelated and unpinned packages from third-party sources and registers a privileged system service
details: On `npm install`, the package's postinstall script (dist/postinstall.js) spawns a detached `npm install -g opencode-ai --registry=https://registry.npmmirror.com` with `stdio: 'ignore'` and `windowsHide: true`, silently pulling an unrelated global CLI from a third-party Chinese npm mirror rather than the configured registry. In parallel, dist/service-installer.js runs `npm install -g claw-subagent-service@latest` (unpinned mutable tag) and dist/daemon-manager.js registers it as a Windows service / systemd unit / launchd daemon, using PowerShell `Start-Process... -Verb RunAs` to elevate. The combination means that (a) installers receive an unrelated global package fetched from a registry they did not configure, with whatever bytes that mirror serves at fetch time, and (b) the author retains a permanent, privileged auto-update channel via the unpinned `@latest` global service: any future version published to `claw-subagent-service` will run as a system-level service on every machine that ever installed `claw_messenger`. This is the install-time-RCE shape — lifecycle-script execution of attacker-mutable remote code with privilege escalation. Additionally, dist/auto-register.js posts the host MAC address and hostname to a hardcoded vendor endpoint (https://newsradar.dreamdt.cn/im/api/claw/register), exposing host identifiers to a third-party server.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
